extraction.one_line_summary: MICA Rafal soloed to victory after establishing dominance on the Puerto de Mejadas, holding off a multi-rider chase group including KRUIJSWIJK Steven by 1:27.
tactical_patterns: MICA Rafal attacked solo on the Puerto de Mejadas (20km at 5.4%) and sustained power gap despite organized chase.
YATES Adam attacked final kilometers to gain symbolic 15 seconds on GC group, targeting teammate BERNAL Egan's position.
Jumbo-Visma controlled peloton despite no GC threat, maintaining unnecessary tempo that exposed unclear Ineos leadership between YATES and BERNAL.
ARU Fabio showed resurrection form in early breakaway, first meaningful Grand Tour aggression in years before retirement announcement.

rider_signals.MICA Rafal: positive: Sustained solo effort over 20km category one climb demonstrates recovered form after early Vuelta struggles
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: positive: First breakaway appearance in years at Grand Tour suggests reconsideration of retirement timeline warranted
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Expended energy for symbolic 15-second gain reveals desperation regarding team hierarchy with BERNAL
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Looking strong without attacking suggests confident waiting game while YATES wastes matches
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: positive: Led chase effort from second-tier group, showing form recovery despite failing to close 1:27 gap
